title: calylophus hartwegii subsp. maccartii
intro: Calylophus hartwegii subsp. maccartii, also known as the pink or orange fairyfan, is a flowering plant species within the Calylophus genus. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Calylophus hartwegii subsp. maccartii include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, and herbal preparations such as infusions and salves can be made from its leaves and flowers.
